leads.name: Wayne State University Press
leads.notes: Wayne State University Press is the academic publishing arm of Wayne State University, founded 1941, producing 40+ titles annually across African American studies, Czech/Slovak studies, film studies, folklore, and Great Lakes regional history with national distribution through SCB Distributors. Detroit sits 45 minutes from Ann Arbor -- the closest major university press to Cushing-Malloy's home base. Institutional budget stability and high annual title volume make this a flagship manufacturing account target. Outreach angle: Midwest academic peer manufacturing partnership for a university press with consistent multi-title annual volume and proximity that enables faster resolution on every production question.

leads.name: University of Michigan Press
leads.notes: The University of Michigan Press is Ann Arbor's own academic publishing arm, producing 100+ titles annually across humanities, social sciences, digital humanities, and regional studies with national distribution. As a fellow Ann Arbor institution, in-person press checks and same-day proof delivery are realistic -- a geographic manufacturing relationship with no peer in the pipeline. Institutional budget stability and consistently high annual title volume make this the highest-value geographic target in any run. Outreach angle: a true Ann Arbor neighbor manufacturing partnership for the university press that shares Cushing-Malloy's city -- one dedicated contact, literally around the corner.

leads.name: Tin House Books
leads.notes: Tin House Books is one of America's most respected independent literary presses, spun off from Tin House magazine in 2005 and producing 20+ titles annually across literary fiction, memoir, and poetry with national distribution through W.W. Norton. Award-winning catalog -- National Book Award nominees, Pulitzer contenders, PEN/Faulkner finalists -- signals a press for whom physical book quality is inseparable from editorial identity. Outreach angle: premium offset literary manufacturing for a press whose 20+ annual titles demand a single manufacturing partner who can execute consistent quality across their full award-winning catalog.

leads.name: Brain Mill Press
leads.notes: Brain Mill Press is a Green Bay, Wisconsin independent literary publisher founded 2014, producing fiction, nonfiction, and poetry with a commitment to underrepresented voices and diverse narratives. Green Bay is approximately 4 hours from Ann Arbor, placing Brain Mill firmly in the Great Lakes corridor. IBPA member with active acquisition program and quality literary production standards. Outreach angle: Midwest peer relationship with a small press that benefits from single-contact manufacturing and offset pricing that outperforms POD for 500-1,000 copy literary runs.

leads.name: Ohio State University Press
leads.notes: Ohio State University Press is the academic publishing arm of one of the largest universities in the US, producing 50+ titles annually across scholarly nonfiction, literary criticism, and regional studies with national distribution. Columbus, Ohio is approximately 2.5 hours from Ann Arbor -- strong geographic proximity in the Great Lakes corridor. Satisfies mandatory education/university source requirement for this run. Outreach angle: Midwest regional manufacturing partnership for an academic press demanding consistent quality across a high-volume annual catalog, with proximity that shortens freight and enables faster resolution.

leads.name: Ohio University Press
leads.notes: Ohio University Press is the academic publishing arm of Ohio University, founded 1947, producing 50+ titles annually across African American studies, Appalachian studies, history, literature, and regional studies through both the OU Press and Swallow Press imprints with national distribution. Athens, Ohio is approximately 3 hours from Ann Arbor -- squarely in the Great Lakes/Ohio corridor. Institutional budget stability, high annual title volume, and dual-imprint structure create consistent manufacturing demand. Outreach angle: Midwest academic peer manufacturing partnership for a dual-imprint university press with 50+ annual titles and geographic proximity that shortens freight and enables faster production resolution.

leads.name: Gnome Road Publishing
leads.notes: Gnome Road Publishing is a Louisville, Kentucky independent children's picture book publisher founded 2019, producing 6-8 titles annually with a focus on whimsical, inclusive stories for young readers. Children's picture books have specialized printing requirements: full-bleed color accuracy, durable binding, and coated stock for high-use durability. IBPA member with active acquisition program. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's color offset capabilities and single-contact spec guidance for a children's publisher whose readers and parents judge every book by its physical quality.

leads.name: McSweeney's
leads.notes: McSweeney's is one of America's most celebrated independent literary publishers, founded by Dave Eggers in 1998 and producing 20+ titles annually across literary fiction, nonfiction, and the acclaimed McSweeney's Quarterly Concern. The press is known for treating the physical book as a design object -- paper, binding, cover treatment, and structural format are all deliberate choices that make manufacturing relationships especially important. National distribution through Consortium. Outreach angle: premium offset manufacturing for a press whose physical book design is inseparable from its literary identity -- a single manufacturing contact who executes specifications consistently across their full annual catalog.

leads.name: Soho Press
leads.notes: Soho Press is a New York independent literary press founded in 1986 by Juris Jurjevics, producing 40+ titles annually across crime fiction, literary fiction, and international literature through the Soho Crime and Soho Teen imprints with national distribution. Their multi-imprint structure signals varied trim sizes and production specifications that benefit from a single dedicated manufacturing contact. Consistent high-volume annual output across three active imprints makes this a meaningful recurring manufacturing account. Outreach angle: single-contact offset manufacturing for a multi-imprint press producing 40+ annual titles where one dedicated contact eliminates the specification drift that occurs with fragmented vendor relationships.

leads.name: Black Lawrence Press
leads.notes: Black Lawrence Press is a New York independent literary press founded 2008, producing 15-20 titles annually across poetry, fiction, and creative nonfiction through multiple active annual contest programs (Black River Chapbook Competition, Hudson Prize, and others). Multi-prize annual calendar creates recurring, predictable print production windows. Strong national distribution. Outreach angle: contest-calendar manufacturing partnership for a press with predictable annual production windows and high annual title volume.

leads.name: Interlude Press
leads.notes: Interlude Press is a New York-area independent publisher specializing in LGBTQ+ literary fiction and romance, producing 8-12 titles annually with national distribution. Dedicated readership values quality physical editions and returns to the press repeatedly. Consistent annual catalog provides recurring print manufacturing opportunities. Outreach angle: single-contact offset manufacturing for a specialty press producing consistent annual title volume in a readership that prizes quality physical editions.

leads.name: Counterpoint Press
leads.notes: Counterpoint Press is a Berkeley, California independent literary publisher founded 1995, producing 30+ titles annually across literary fiction, nonfiction, and essays through the Counterpoint and Soft Skull Press imprints with national distribution. Their dual-imprint structure covering both literary and experimental fiction signals varied annual production volumes and specifications. Counterpoint titles have received National Book Award recognition and consistent critical acclaim. Outreach angle: reliable offset manufacturing for a dual-imprint Berkeley press producing 30+ annual titles -- one dedicated contact who knows their production standards across both imprints, title after title.

leads.name: BkMk Press
leads.notes: BkMk Press (Book Mark Press) is the literary publisher of the University of Missouri-Kansas City, founded 1971, producing poetry, short fiction, and essays through an active publishing program. Over 50 years in operation, BkMk produces 6-8 titles annually with national distribution. University-affiliated, institutionally stable, and with consistent annual title volume. Cushing-Malloy was already 23 years old when BkMk was founded -- a peer longevity story. Outreach angle: Midwest institutional manufacturing partnership for a 50-year press that deserves a manufacturer with the same longevity and reliability.

leads.name: Braddock Avenue Books
leads.notes: Braddock Avenue Books is a Pittsburgh-area independent literary press producing fiction, poetry, and essays with a working-class literary sensibility rooted in the industrial heritage of western Pennsylvania. CLMP member with active acquisitions and national distribution. Editorial identity built on craft, labor, and the working-class experience aligns naturally with Cushing-Malloy's manufacturing heritage. Outreach angle: a manufacturing partner whose identity matches their editorial values -- one dedicated contact, no dropped details, books produced with the same craft the press champions.

leads.name: Gold Wake Press
leads.notes: Gold Wake Press is a Des Moines, Iowa nonprofit literary publisher producing poetry and innovative fiction with an annual publication cycle. Iowa is firmly in Cushing-Malloy's Midwest reach. Nonprofit structure means per-unit cost efficiency matters significantly. At typical poetry print runs of 300-750 copies, Cushing-Malloy's short-run offset often outperforms POD on both quality and per-unit cost. Outreach angle: competitive short-run poetry and fiction pricing with print run economics education for a Midwest nonprofit press.

leads.name: Unnamed Press
leads.notes: Unnamed Press is a Los Angeles-based independent literary publisher focused on international literary fiction in translation, with a growing catalog distributed nationally through Publishers Group West. Translated literary fiction often involves complex typographic layouts, diacritical marks, and specific trim and paper requirements. Outreach angle: precise offset production for a press whose translated titles demand a manufacturer who can execute complex typographic specifications consistently.

leads.name: New Directions
leads.notes: New Directions is one of America's most prestigious and historically important independent literary publishers, founded by James Laughlin in 1936 and producing 50+ titles annually of international literature in translation, literary fiction, poetry, and essays. Their catalog includes Nobel Prize laureates, Booker Prize winners, and generations of defining literary voices. National distribution with a globally recognized backlist and consistent new title production. Outreach angle: premium offset literary manufacturing for one of America's oldest and most respected independent presses -- a single manufacturing contact who can maintain the production standards their Nobel-level catalog demands across 50+ annual titles.

leads.name: Neon Hemlock Press
leads.notes: Neon Hemlock Press is a Washington, DC-based independent publisher specializing in queer, diverse, and innovative speculative fiction -- novellas, chapbooks, and short fiction anthologies. Dedicated queer SFF readership drives consistent annual title production. Short novellas and chapbooks have specific binding and paper requirements that offset handles better than POD at their print volumes. Outreach angle: short-run offset for a speculative fiction press whose distinctive physical editions are as much a part of their brand identity as the content.

leads.name: Torrey House Press
leads.notes: Torrey House Press is Salt Lake City, Utah's leading nonprofit environmental literary publisher, founded 2010 and producing 11 titles in 2026 at the intersection of literary arts and environmental advocacy. National distribution with a sustainability-conscious readership. Production choices matter to their readers -- paper stock, binding, and printing method are part of the value proposition. Outreach angle: quality offset manufacturing for a nonprofit press whose production choices need to reflect the environmental values they publish about.

leads.name: Zibby Books
leads.notes: Zibby Books is a New York boutique publisher founded by media entrepreneur Zibby Owens, producing 10-15 titles annually in literary fiction and memoir. Their Book Champion model -- established authors mentoring debut authors with an equity stake -- creates strong author-community investment in each title's physical quality. Active 2026 catalog with multiple titles launched this year. Outreach angle: quality offset production for a boutique press whose author-community model drives higher-touch expectations around the physical book.
